object_storage.notes: PAYG: €0.04/GB overage. 5TB plan: €120/mo (€0.024/GB overage). 20TB plan: €400/mo (€0.02/GB overage). Incoming traffic and CDN egress free.

egress.notes: Free egress traffic included on all instance types

gcore.notes: Luxembourg-based cloud. Prices from Luxembourg-2 region. Free K8s control plane. Free DDoS protection included. Prices vary by location. New lower prices announced for Frankfurt-2, Warsaw, Paris-2, Santa-Clara, Tokyo, Mumbai, Johannesburg, Sao Paulo-2.

scaleup.source: Preisliste ScaleUp Open Cloud 2026-03, Preisliste OpenStack 2022-07

egress.notes: Free with fair use policy

scaleup.notes: Pricing is resource-based: 0.02€/h per CPU core, 0.005€/h per GB RAM (730h/month). Based on Sovereign Cloud Stack (SCS). K8s non-HA: 0.10€/h (73€/month), K8s HA: 0.30€/h (219€/month). Resource packages available with 12-month commitment.

egress.notes: Egress included in instance price

object_storage.notes: Standard €0.01/GB, One Zone €0.012/GB, Glacier €0.002/GB. 750GB free for 90 days.

egress.notes: Unlimited traffic included with 50 Mbps non-tariffed bandwidth

serverspace.notes: vStack cloud with pay-as-you-go billing in 10-minute increments. Fixed plans for Linux shown. Kubernetes control plane: €21.85/mo (standard), €65.55/mo (HA). Locations: Amsterdam, Toronto, Dubai, Almaty, New Jersey, Sao Paulo, Tashkent (Uzbekistan).

kubernetes.name: SKE (STACKIT Kubernetes Engine)
kubernetes.notes: Control plane €74.63/month + worker node compute costs

block_storage.notes: Pricing varies by performance class (perf-0 to perf-6)

egress.notes: Outgoing data traffic is currently free of charge

stackit.data_residency: Germany and Austria only (EU data sovereignty)
stackit.notes: Set STACKIT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN and STACKIT_PROJECT_ID for live API data. Static prices from official price list v1.0.38.

object_storage.notes: Billed per GB-hour. Free egress. German data sovereignty (Schwarz Group).

egress.notes: Data transfer free for all network types: private, cross-AZ, inbound, outbound

thalassa.notes: Dutch cloud provider. DGP (development) uses Intel Xeon E5, PGP (production) uses Intel Xeon Gold. Per-minute billing.

upcloud.notes: Set UPCLOUD_USERNAME and UPCLOUD_PASSWORD env vars for live data

load_balancer.notes: No bandwidth limits, SSL offloading, HTTP3, caching. Danish datacenter.

block_storage.notes: Block storage not available. Disk included with instances.

egress.notes: 1-7TB included depending on plan. Fair usage policy.

object_storage.notes: Object storage (S3) not available. Coming soon per their roadmap.
